_metadata.description: Machine-readable validation rules for the ConstructionDailyLog schema. Consumed directly by: dataset generators (Sprint 2), AI extraction validator (Sprint 4), API input validation (Sprint 7). Each rule is self-contained with condition, severity, and remediation guidance.

categories.SEQ: Stage sequencing — is the stated stage possible given the project history?
categories.MAT: Material consistency — do the materials match the stated stage?
categories.WRK: Worker consistency — do the workforce records make logical sense?
categories.WTH: Weather-work consistency — does work match weather conditions?
categories.QTY: Quantity sanity — are numeric values within realistic bounds?
categories.DTE: Date consistency — are dates valid and internally consistent?
categories.INS: Inspection sequence — are inspections occurring in the correct order?
categories.SAF: Safety consistency — are safety records complete and consistent?
categories.FIN: Financial consistency — do financial values make sense?

severity_definitions.blocking: Record should not be stored. Indicates fabricated or deeply corrupted data.
severity_definitions.non_blocking_error: Record can be stored as draft. Must be reviewed and corrected before approval.
severity_definitions.warning: Record can be approved with explicit override. Flags unusual but possible situations.
severity_definitions.info: Advisory only. Stores as note. Does not block approval.

applies_to_values.dataset_generation: Rule applied by Sprint 2 dataset generators when creating synthetic data.
applies_to_values.ai_extraction: Rule applied by Sprint 4 AI extraction engine after parsing voice note.
applies_to_values.api_input: Rule applied by Sprint 7 FastAPI endpoint on incoming JSON from mobile app or web.
applies_to_values.approval_workflow: Rule applied when a reviewer tries to approve a draft log.

rules.name: Painting Requires Drywall
rules.description: A log recording painting work implies drywall is complete. If the current_stage is painting, prior stages must include drywall completion.
rules.error_message: Stage 'painting' is recorded but stage 'drywall' has not been completed. Painting requires finished drywall surfaces.
rules.suggested_fix: Verify the current_stage is correct. If painting is happening, drywall must be marked complete in project history.
rules.notes: Most commonly violated by AI extraction when foreman says 'we primed walls' during late drywall stage.

rules.name: Roofing Requires Framing
rules.description: Roofing work cannot happen before framing is substantially complete.
rules.error_message: Stage 'roofing' recorded but 'framing' is not substantially complete. Cannot roof without a structural frame.
rules.suggested_fix: Check if current_stage was mislabeled. If roofing is truly happening, framing must be substantially done.

rules.name: Rough-In Requires Framing
rules.description: Electrical, HVAC, or plumbing rough-in cannot happen before framing.
rules.error_message: Rough-in stage '{current_stage}' recorded but framing is not substantially complete.
rules.suggested_fix: Verify stage. Rough-in trades follow framing.
rules.notes: Electrical can sometimes start on a completed section while framing continues elsewhere.

rules.name: Drywall Requires All Rough-In Inspections
rules.description: Drywall requires all three rough-in trades to be complete and inspected.
rules.error_message: Drywall recorded but not all rough-in inspections confirmed passed. Inspections required: electrical, plumbing, HVAC, insulation.
rules.suggested_fix: Add inspection records showing all rough-in inspections passed before drywall date.
rules.notes: Validation at AI extraction level should flag this as warning since the AI cannot access project history.

rules.name: Flooring After Painting
rules.description: Flooring should not be installed before painting is substantially complete in the same area.
rules.error_message: Flooring recorded but painting is not yet substantially complete. Risk of paint damage to flooring.
rules.suggested_fix: Verify sequencing. Note if flooring and painting are in different rooms.
rules.notes: Most common in dataset generation. Tile work is the exception — can happen before painting.

rules.name: Finishing After Drywall
rules.description: Trim, millwork, cabinets, and countertops cannot happen before drywall.
rules.error_message: Finishing work (trim/cabinets) recorded before drywall is complete.
rules.suggested_fix: Verify stage. Trim and cabinets require finished wall surfaces.

rules.name: Concrete During Non-Concrete Stage
rules.description: Large quantities of concrete/cement should only appear during foundation or flatwork stages.
rules.error_message: Concrete material recorded during stage '{current_stage}'. Verify this is correct (small repairs are acceptable).
rules.suggested_fix: If this is repair work, note it in the task description. If it's a data extraction error, correct the material entry.
rules.notes: Cement bags used in small quantities for patching or anchoring are acceptable.

rules.name: Roofing Materials During Non-Roofing Stage
rules.description: Shingles, underlayment, and roofing-specific flashing should only appear during roofing.
rules.error_message: Roofing materials (shingles/underlayment) recorded during stage '{current_stage}'. This is unexpected.
rules.suggested_fix: Correct the material entry or the stage. Roofing materials belong to the roofing stage.

rules.name: Electrical Materials Require Electrical Stage
rules.description: Electrical wire (NM cable, conduit, AFCI breakers) should only appear during electrical rough-in or finish stages.
rules.error_message: Electrical materials recorded during stage '{current_stage}'. Unexpected unless this is minor repair work.
rules.suggested_fix: Verify stage. Electrical materials belong to electrical rough-in or finish stages.

rules.name: Plumbing Materials Require Plumbing Stage
rules.description: PVC pipe, copper pipe, PEX tubing should appear during plumbing rough-in or finish stages.
rules.error_message: Plumbing materials (PVC/copper/PEX pipe) recorded during stage '{current_stage}'.
rules.suggested_fix: Verify material entry. Plumbing materials belong to plumbing rough-in or finish.

rules.name: Paint During Non-Painting Stage
rules.description: Paint (gallons, primer, finish coats) should only appear during painting stage.
rules.error_message: Paint materials recorded during stage '{current_stage}'. Verify this is correct.
rules.suggested_fix: Verify. Small amounts of paint for touch-ups are acceptable during finishing stages.

rules.name: Drywall Materials Require Drywall Stage
rules.description: Gypsum board, joint compound, and paper tape are exclusively drywall stage materials.
rules.error_message: Drywall materials recorded during stage '{current_stage}'.
rules.suggested_fix: Verify stage. If drywall work is happening, set current_stage to 'drywall'.

rules.name: Zero Workers But Work Completed
rules.description: Work cannot be completed without workers present.

condition.logic: IF A AND B THEN INVALID

rules.error_message: work_completed has entries but total_workers_present is 0. Work requires workers.
rules.suggested_fix: Either set total_workers_present > 0 or remove work_completed entries.

rules.name: Late Arrivals Exceed Total Workers
rules.description: Cannot have more late arrivals than total workers present.
rules.error_message: Late arrivals count ({count}) exceeds total workers present ({total}). Impossible.
rules.suggested_fix: Check worker counts. Late arrivals is a subset of workers present.

rules.name: Unrealistic Worker Count for Residential
rules.description: Residential projects very rarely exceed 30 workers simultaneously.
rules.error_message: Total workers ({count}) is unusually high for a single-family residential project. Maximum realistic is ~30.
rules.suggested_fix: Verify count. If correct, add a note explaining the high count.
rules.notes: Dataset generators should use trade-specific typical worker counts from construction_stages.json.

rules.name: Man Hours Reasonable for Worker Count
rules.description: Total man hours should be between workers × 4 and workers × 12.
rules.error_message: Total man hours ({hours}) is outside expected range for {workers} workers ({min_expected}–{max_expected} hours).
rules.suggested_fix: Verify man hours. For {workers} workers, expect approximately {min_expected}–{max_expected} hours total.

rules.name: Concrete Pour During Rain
rules.description: Concrete cannot be poured during rain. This is a quality and structural violation.
rules.error_message: Concrete pour work recorded during rain conditions. Invalid — rain compromises concrete quality.
rules.suggested_fix: Remove concrete pour from work_completed, or correct the weather condition. These are mutually exclusive.
rules.notes: AI should check if the pour happened in the morning before rain started — if so, acceptable.

rules.name: Roofing During Rain
rules.description: Shingles and underlayment cannot be applied during rain. Safety and adhesion issue.
rules.error_message: Roofing work recorded during precipitation. Invalid — wet surfaces prevent shingle adhesion.
rules.suggested_fix: If roofing happened in the afternoon after rain cleared, use afternoon_condition to determine validity.

rules.name: Weather Stoppage Without Delay Entry
rules.description: If weather caused work to stop, a delay entry with type 'weather' must exist.
rules.error_message: weather.work_stopped_due_to_weather is true but no weather delay entry found in delays array.
rules.suggested_fix: Add a delay entry with delay_type 'weather' to document the work stoppage.

rules.name: Rain Frequency Too High in Dataset
rules.description: Statistical rule for dataset generation only. Rain should not appear in more than 30% of logs.
rules.error_message: Rain condition appears in {percent}% of logs for this project. Exceeds realistic maximum of 30% (excluding rainy climates).
rules.suggested_fix: Adjust dataset generator weather probability weights.
rules.notes: Only applicable to dataset generation. Not a validation error for real logs.

rules.name: Concrete Volume Sanity Check
rules.description: Single residential concrete pour should not exceed 100 cubic yards.
rules.error_message: Concrete volume ({value} cubic yards) exceeds typical residential single pour of 100 CY. Verify units (bags vs cubic yards?).
rules.suggested_fix: Verify units. 40 bags of concrete ≠ 40 cubic yards. 1 bag = ~0.5 cubic foot = 0.018 cubic yards.
rules.notes: AI often confuses bags and cubic yards. This rule catches that extraction error.

rules.name: Completion Percent Range
rules.description: stage_completion_percent and overall_project_completion_percent must be between 0 and 100.
rules.error_message: Completion percentage ({value}) is outside valid range 0–100.
rules.suggested_fix: Set completion percentage to a value between 0 and 100.

rules.name: Overall Completion Should Not Decrease
rules.description: Overall project completion should not decrease from one log to the next unless rework is documented.
rules.error_message: Overall completion decreased from {previous}% to {current}% without a rework delay entry.
rules.suggested_fix: Add a rework delay entry to explain the decrease, or correct the completion percentage.
rules.notes: Only applicable when comparing across multiple logs in the same project.

rules.name: Steel Rods Quantity Sanity
rules.description: Steel rods/rebar reported in a single day should not exceed realistic framing quantities.
rules.error_message: Steel rods quantity ({value} each) is unusually high. Verify this is correct.
rules.suggested_fix: Verify quantity. For typical residential foundation, 200-800 rebar pieces is realistic.

rules.name: Log Date Not in Future
rules.description: A daily log cannot be created for a future date.
rules.error_message: log_date ({log_date}) is in the future. Daily logs can only be created for past or current dates.
rules.suggested_fix: Set log_date to today or a past date.
rules.notes: Does not apply to dataset generation (generating historical data is fine).

rules.name: Log Date After Project Start
rules.description: Log date must be on or after the project start date.
rules.error_message: log_date ({log_date}) is before project_start_date ({start_date}). Daily logs cannot predate project start.
rules.suggested_fix: Verify log_date or project_start_date. One of them is incorrect.

rules.name: Log Date Not Excessively Beyond Planned Completion
rules.description: Logs should not be created more than 6 months beyond the planned completion date without explanation.
rules.error_message: log_date ({log_date}) is more than 6 months beyond planned_completion_date ({completion_date}). Project significantly over schedule.
rules.suggested_fix: Update planned_completion_date to reflect current schedule, or verify log_date is correct.

rules.name: Inspection Result is Valid
rules.description: Inspection result must be one of the valid enum values.
rules.error_message: Inspection result '{value}' is not a valid value. Must be one of: passed, failed, conditional_pass, partial_pass, cancelled, rescheduled, pending.
rules.suggested_fix: Set result to one of the valid enum values.

rules.name: Failed Inspection Requires Corrections List
rules.description: A failed inspection should include at least one correction item.
rules.error_message: Inspection marked as 'failed' but corrections_required is empty. A failed inspection must list what needs to be corrected.
rules.suggested_fix: Add at least one correction item to inspections[*].corrections_required.

rules.name: Drywall Stage Inspection Prerequisites
rules.description: For dataset generation: before any drywall log, the project history must include passed inspections for rough electrical, rough plumbing, rough HVAC, and insulation.
rules.error_message: Generating drywall-stage log but project history lacks required inspection passes.
rules.suggested_fix: Ensure dataset generator inserts inspection-passed logs before generating drywall logs.
rules.notes: Critical rule for Sprint 2 dataset generators.

rules.name: OSHA Recordable Requires Review
rules.description: Any log containing an OSHA-recordable incident must be set to under_review status.
rules.error_message: Log contains OSHA recordable incident but review_status is '{status}'. Must be under_review or approved.
rules.suggested_fix: Set review_status to 'under_review' for OSHA recordable incidents. A supervisor must review.

rules.name: Hard Hat Required on All Sites
rules.description: Hard hat should always be listed in required PPE regardless of stage.
rules.error_message: hard_hat not found in ppe_required_today. Hard hats are required on all construction sites.
rules.suggested_fix: Add 'hard_hat' to safety.ppe_required_today.
rules.notes: OSHA 29 CFR 1926.100 requires hard hats where there is risk of head injury.

rules.name: Daily Total Cost Matches Sum
rules.description: daily_total_cost_usd should equal sum of labor + material + equipment + subcontractor costs.
rules.error_message: daily_total_cost_usd ({total}) does not match sum of component costs ({calculated_total}).
rules.suggested_fix: Recalculate daily_total_cost_usd as the sum of all daily cost fields.
rules.notes: Only relevant when financial fields are populated. Financials are optional in Sprint 1-5.

rule_execution_order.description: Recommended execution order for rule validation. Blocking rules first — fail fast.
